pattern: Treating each life span as a plus-one at birth and minus-one at death, a difference-array prefix sum over the year range reveals the earliest year with peak overlap.
hints: A person contributes to every year from birth up to but not including death.
Mark a delta of plus-one at the birth year and minus-one at the death year, then accumulate across years.
Run a running sum over the fixed year window; the first year that hits the maximum running total is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: two non-overlapping spans, peak is one
tie on peak, choose the earlier year
death year excluded so no overlap despite touching
spans at the extreme year bounds

stress.description: many random life spans within the legal year window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    logs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        b = rng.randint(1950, 2049)
        d = rng.randint(b + 1, 2050)
        logs.append([b, d])
    return [logs]
